title: Fork termux/termux-packages and retarget $PREFIX to dev.warp.mobile

script_commit.subject: scripts: setup-warp-prefix.sh — retarget PREFIX from upstream Termux to Warp on Mobile

retarget_summary.symlinks_skipped_reason: BSD sed -i refuses to edit symlinked targets; the symlink reflects changes once its target (packages/neovim/nvim-shim.sh) is rewritten

audit.audit_method: git ls-files | xargs grep -l (avoids grep -rln buffering issues on 9700-file tree)
audit_intentional_preservations: *.md (CONTRIBUTING.md and friends — keep upstream traceability references)
*.c (vendored upstream sources in disabled-packages, not in active build target)
*.gn (vendored swiftshader configs in chromium / electron third_party_override; complex per-package handling)
audit.github_com_urls_preserved_note: Sed pattern correctly escapes literal dots so 'com.termux' does not match 'com/termux' in URLs like https://github.com/termux/termux-packages.

key_implementation_notes.sed_flavor_autodetect: GNU sed and BSD/macOS sed both supported
key_implementation_notes.self_protection: OLD_PKG / OLD_PREFIX / OLD_HOME defined via base64 decode so the script does not literally contain the search target; script's own path also excluded from candidate list
key_implementation_notes.discovery_via_git_ls_files: git ls-files used for hit detection in git checkouts; falls back to find for non-git environments (CI raw extracts)
key_implementation_notes.regex_dot_escaping: All literal dots in OLD_PKG / OLD_PREFIX / OLD_HOME escaped before sed substitution to avoid matching 'com/termux' inside URLs (e.g. github.com/termux/...)

smoke_test.reason: Docker not installed on macOS host (executor environment); termux-packages bash bootstrap requires Linux/Docker container. Defer to M4-S03 (bootstrap-aarch64.zip build) where Docker setup is the prerequisite.
smoke_test.documentation_path: termux-packages/README.md (upstream) and termux-packages/scripts/run-docker.sh — both retargeted but not exercised in this story

bootstrap_artifact_path.expected_output_dir: termux-packages/output/ (and the per-arch boot strap layout under debs/)
bootstrap_artifact_path.note: M4-S02 does NOT produce a bootstrap zip. Path is documented here so M4-S03 can pick it up; actual zip generation is an M4-S03 acceptance criterion.

files_touched: README.md (added step 2b: clone termux-packages fork + warp-mobile/main checkout, plus directory tree update)
main_repo_changes.main_repo_commit_hash: PENDING — committed after this result.json is finalized

risks_for_m4_s03.title: Docker not installed on executor host
risks_for_m4_s03.detail: termux-packages/scripts/run-docker.sh is the canonical path for building bootstrap zips. M4-S03 must either install Docker on macOS (Docker Desktop or colima) or provision a Linux build host.

risks_for_m4_s03.title: 13 com.termux references remain in non-build files
risks_for_m4_s03.detail: Mostly *.md docs (intentional) and vendored *.c / *.gn / generated cache. Some (notably packages/libandroid-wordexp/wordexp.c and disabled-packages/termux-x11/termux-x11.c) may need per-package attention if those packages enter the M4 bootstrap target. Disabled packages are likely safe to ignore.

risks_for_m4_s03.title: TERMUX_APP__PACKAGE_NAME alone may be insufficient — patches now hardcode dev.warp.mobile
risks_for_m4_s03.detail: Previously, scripts/properties.sh's TERMUX_APP__PACKAGE_NAME was the single source of truth. We have now also rewritten 32 *.patch files. If we ever want to upstream changes back to termux/termux-packages, those patches would need to be reverted (or generated dynamically) before submission. This is acceptable for a downstream fork.

risks_for_m4_s03.title: oma package uses runtime sed-replace logic
risks_for_m4_s03.detail: packages/oma/build.sh constructs 'com.termux' programmatically via string concatenation and sed-replaces it at build time using TERMUX_APP__PACKAGE_NAME (which we set to dev.warp.mobile). This works automatically and was not modified. Spot-check: build correctly during M4-S03.

gate.overall_pass_caveat: Codex verifier review pending (lead-dispatched per CRITICAL SOP — executor MUST NOT self-dispatch).
